title: id-translation configuration
description: Schema for the id-translation main configuration file. The fixed grammar is validated; built-in fetcher types are enumerated, while custom (fully-qualified) types, function kwargs and overrides stay permissive because their keys depend on the chosen implementation. See [the configuration docs](https://id-translation.readthedocs.io/en/stable/documentation/translator-config.html).

fmt.description: Format string controlling how translated IDs are displayed, e.g. `{id}:{name}`.

enable_uuid_heuristics.description: Improve matching when UUID-like IDs are in use.

unknownIds.description: Display and placeholder values for IDs that could not be translated. See [the docs](https://id-translation.readthedocs.io/en/stable/documentation/translator-config.html#section-unknown-ids).

fmt.description: Format for untranslated IDs. A plain string like `Unknown`, or `{id}` to leave the ID as-is.

transform.description: One `Transformer` per source: `[transform.<source>.<TransformerType>]`. The innermost keys are passed to the transformer's `__init__`. `<TransformerType>` may be a built-in name or a fully-qualified type name. Transformers belong to the `Translator`, but may live in an auxiliary fetcher file too (co-located with the fetcher that produces the source); a given source may be transformed only once across all files. See [the docs](https://id-translation.readthedocs.io/en/stable/documentation/translator-config.html#section-transformations).

fetching.description: Fetcher configuration. Exactly one implementation block is expected (`[fetching.<FetcherType>]`: a built-in name or a fully-qualified type), alongside the optional reserved `mapping`, `cache` and `MultiFetcher` sections. See [the docs](https://id-translation.readthedocs.io/en/stable/documentation/translator-config.html#section-fetching).

fetcherCommon.description: Keyword arguments accepted by every `AbstractFetcher` implementation.

allow_fetch_all.description: Control access to `Fetcher.fetch_all`. Some fetcher types redefine or ignore this key.

selective_fetch_all.description: Sources without required keys are not fetched.

identifiers.description: Hierarchical identifiers for the fetcher. Based on the source file if not given.

optional.description: Multi-fetcher mode only: if true, discard the fetcher on a sources-resolution crash.

fetcherImpl.description: A custom fetcher block keyed by fully-qualified type name (e.g. `[fetching.'my.library.SuperFetcher']`). All keys are forwarded to the constructor.

data.description: In-memory translation data, keyed by source. Usually supplied programmatically rather than in TOML.

return_all.description: Return all available data for fetch-all operations. Default true.

read_function.description: Pandas read function, e.g. `read_csv`. Inferred from `read_path_format` if omitted.

read_path_format.description: Format string for source paths, e.g. `data/{}.csv`. Default `data/{}.csv`.

read_function_kwargs.description: Extra keyword arguments forwarded to the read function.

password.description: Password injected into the connection string (keeps it out of the string itself).

whitelist_tables.description: If given, only these tables are eligible as sources.

schema.description: Database schema to read tables from.

include_views.description: Include views in addition to tables. Default false.

cache.description: User-defined `CacheAccess`. Only `type` is required; all other keys are forwarded to the implementation's `__init__`. See [choosing a caching strategy](https://id-translation.readthedocs.io/en/stable/documentation/translator-config.html#choosing-a-cache).

multiFetcher.description: `MultiFetcher` options. Permitted only in the main configuration file. See [the docs](https://id-translation.readthedocs.io/en/stable/documentation/translator-config.html#multiple-fetchers).

max_workers.description: Maximum number of child fetchers to call in parallel.

on_source_conflict.description: Action for disputes during source discovery.

fetcher_discarded_log_level.description: Log level used when discarding optional fetchers that fail during source discovery.

mapping.description: Mapping configuration (`[*.mapping]`). Known keys are typed; additional `Mapper` keyword arguments are allowed. See [the docs](https://id-translation.readthedocs.io/en/stable/documentation/translator-config.html#subsection-mapping).

score_function.description: Score-function name, or a table of `<name>.<kwargs>` for score functions that take arguments.

on_unmapped.description: How to handle values that could not be matched to a candidate.

cardinality.description: How many candidates a single value may map to.

functionEntry.description: A function reference plus implementation-specific keyword arguments.

function.description: Function name (built-in) or fully-qualified name in single quotes.

heuristicEntry.description: A heuristic-function reference plus implementation-specific keyword arguments.

function.description: Function name (built-in) or fully-qualified name in single quotes.

mutate.description: Keep changes made by `function`. Disabled by default.

overrides.description: Shared and context-specific key-value overrides (`InheritedKeysDict`). No fixed keys: shared items at the top level, context-specific items in `[*.overrides.<context>]` subsections. See [the docs](https://id-translation.readthedocs.io/en/stable/documentation/translator-config.html#subsection-overrides).
